Will Jacks, right-arm off break, comes into the attack

Will Jacks to Shubman Gill, FOUR, top shot! Gill makes room and cuts the ball that was on the stumps. Times it well to beat the point fielder and runs away. The fielder gives chase and tries to pull it back in, but the slope ensures the ball hits the fence

Atkinson to Rohit Sharma, no run, big swing and a miss. Rohit wanted to hit the back of a length delivery to orbit. Missed by a long way

Atkinson to Rohit Sharma, no run, appeal for LBW! That looked like it was going over the stumps. Nips into the right-hander and hits Rohit's back thigh on the attempted pull shot. England decide not to review

Atkinson to Rohit Sharma, FOUR, Rohit was desperate for a boundary and gets one this ball. He moves a long way across his stumps and paddles it fine. Fielder in the deep was nowhere in the picture to stop that

Atkinson to Rohit Sharma, FOUR, fifty for Rohit. His 63rd in ODIs. Clever shot from Rohit. Goes across yet again and picks the fuller length delivery early. Scoops it fine again and gets to the milestone. Raises his bat to the crowd, who applaud the effort

England review! It was a confident appeal for LBW. Curran was almost pleading with the umpire. They don't waste much time in challenging Martin Saggers' decision. The impact seemed to be an issue. There's nothing on UltraEdge. Over to ball-tracking - wickets is umpire's call. Not Out

Sam Curran to Rohit Sharma, no run, that looked closer than it appeared. England don't lose the review. 77ks moon ball from Curran, dips viciously on Rohit, who was kneeling down to play the slog sweep, the ball sneaks under his bat and strikes him on the back leg. The impact was on off-stump and the angle was making it just clip the outside of off-stump..

Sam Curran to Rohit Sharma, FOUR, that's how you do it. Another very slow delivery from Curran, Rohit does not lose his shape, waits for the ball to arrive and lofts through the line. He gets more height and the ball lands just short of the long-off fence

Jacob Bethell to Rohit Sharma, SIX, that's the Rohit we all know. Bethell loses his length and lands it in the arc for Rohit, he sits down and launches the slog sweep into the deep mid-wicket stand. Once again chants of Rohit, Rohit at Lord's
Jacob Bethell to Rohit Sharma, THATS OUT!! Bowled!!

Jacob Bethell to Rohit Sharma, out Bowled!! Got him! Bethell strikes back with the big wicket. He and Buttler are pumped, so are the rest of the England players. A magnificent knock comes to an end. Kohli pats him on the helmet as he walks past and the entire Lord's crowd stand to applaud. What a knock! He has kept India alive in this series decider. Not much runs in the first two games, in fact in the last few games and there was pressure mounting, but Rohit has silenced all that with a fabulous knock. Bethell does not lose his calm. He trusts what he's doing and delivers the wicket. Drifts it in with the arm, Rohit shuffles across and sits down to play the sweep, misses and the ball brushes the pad before crashing into middle-stump. Rohit Sharma b Jacob Bethell 138(110) [4s-17 6s-5]
